raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J123",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The agent output maps against the nearest works (Formaggia and Perotto, Becker and Rannacher, Frey and Alauzet) and identifies sharp deltas between GoalAniso and prior art.",
    "R1.7": "The agent flags missing or wrong citations (e.g., Formaggia–Perotto 2003), audits every load-bearing citation, and demonstrates bibliographic precision.",
    "RX.1": "The agent marks confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation, including uncertainty about the Formaggia–Perotto framing and the need to verify it.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without drift or scope creep.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; the agent identifies potential issues (e.g., cost, title/name uniqueness) and provides recommendations to address them.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including the rationale for revising the original text."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.98,
    "RX.4": 0.92,
    "RX.5": 0.99
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The agent output maps against the nearest works (Formaggia and Perotto, Becker and Rannacher, Frey and Alauzet) and identifies sharp deltas between GoalAniso and prior art.
rationale.R1.7: The agent flags missing or wrong citations (e.g., Formaggia–Perotto 2003), audits every load-bearing citation, and demonstrates bibliographic precision.
rationale.RX.1: The agent marks confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation, including uncertainty about the Formaggia–Perotto framing and the need to verify it.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d) without drift or scope creep.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; the agent identifies potential issues (e.g., cost, title/name uniqueness) and provides recommendations to address them.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including the rationale for revising the original text.
